NKJV.1: A Song of Ascents. In my distress I cried to the LORD, And He heard me.
NKJV.2: Deliver my soul, O LORD, from lying lips And from a deceitful tongue.
NKJV.3: What shall be given to you, Or what shall be done to you, You false tongue?
NKJV.4: Sharp arrows of the warrior, With coals of the broom tree!
NKJV.5: Woe is me, that I dwell in Meshech, That I dwell among the tents of Kedar!
NKJV.6: My soul has dwelt too long With one who hates peace.
NKJV.7: I am for peace; But when I speak, they are for war.

KJV.1: A Song<S>7892</S> of degrees<S>4609</S>. In my distress<S>6869</S> I cried<S>7121</S> unto the LORD<S>3068</S>, and he heard<S>6030</S> me.
KJV.2: Deliver<S>5337</S> my soul<S>5315</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>, from lying<S>8267</S> lips<S>8193</S>, and from a deceitful<S>7423</S> tongue<S>3956</S>.
KJV.3: What shall be given<S>5414</S> unto thee? or what shall be done<S>3254</S> unto thee, thou false<S>7423</S> tongue<S>3956</S>? <sup>What shall be given: or, What shall the deceitful tongue give unto thee? or, what shall is profit thee?</sup> <sup>done: Heb. added</sup>
KJV.4: Sharp<S>8150</S> arrows<S>2671</S> of the mighty<S>1368</S>, with coals<S>1513</S> of juniper<S>7574</S>. <sup>Sharp: or, It is as the sharp arrows of the mighty man, with coals of juniper</sup>
KJV.5: Woe<S>190</S> is me, that I sojourn<S>1481</S> in Mesech<S>4902</S>, that I dwell<S>7931</S> in the tents<S>168</S> of Kedar<S>6938</S>!
KJV.6: My soul<S>5315</S> hath long<S>7227</S> dwelt<S>7931</S> with him that hateth<S>8130</S> peace<S>7965</S>.
KJV.7: I am for peace<S>7965</S>: but when I speak<S>1696</S>, they are for war<S>4421</S>. <sup>for peace: or, a man of peace</sup>

ESV.1: In my distress I called to the LORD, and he answered me.
ESV.2: Deliver me, O LORD, from lying lips, from a deceitful tongue.
ESV.3: What shall be given to you, and what more shall be done to you, you deceitful tongue?
ESV.4: A warrior's sharp arrows, with glowing coals of the broom tree!
ESV.5: Woe to me, that I sojourn in Meshech, that I dwell among the tents of Kedar!
ESV.6: Too long have I had my dwelling among those who hate peace.
ESV.7: I am for peace, but when I speak, they are for war!

NASB.1: A Song of Ascents. In my trouble I cried to the LORD, And He answered me.
NASB.2: Deliver my soul, O LORD, from lying lips, From a deceitful tongue.
NASB.3: What shall be given to you, and what more shall be done to you, You deceitful tongue?
NASB.4: Sharp arrows of the warrior, With the [burning] coals of the broom tree.
NASB.5: Woe is me, for I sojourn in Meshech, For I dwell among the tents of Kedar!
NASB.6: Too long has my soul had its dwelling With those who hate peace.
NASB.7: I am [for] peace, but when I speak, They are for war.

NLT.1:
I took my troubles to the LORD;
I cried out to him, and he answered my prayer.
NLT.2:
Rescue me, O LORD, from liars
and from all deceitful people.
NLT.3:
O deceptive tongue, what will God do to you?
How will he increase your punishment?
NLT.4:
You will be pierced with sharp arrows
and burned with glowing coals.
NLT.5:
How I suffer in far-off Meshech.
It pains me to live in distant Kedar.
NLT.6:
I am tired of living
among people who hate peace.
NLT.7:
I search for peace;
but when I speak of peace, they want war!

WEB.1: A Song of Ascents. In my distress, I cried to Yahweh. He answered me.
WEB.2: Deliver my soul, Yahweh, from lying lips, from a deceitful tongue.
WEB.3: What will be given to you, and what will be done more to you, you deceitful tongue?
WEB.4: Sharp arrows of the mighty, with coals of juniper.
WEB.5: Woe is me, that I live in Meshech, that I dwell among the tents of Kedar!
WEB.6: My soul has had her dwelling too long with him who hates peace.
WEB.7: I am for peace, but when I speak, they are for war.

AMP.1:
A Song of Ascents. In my trouble I cried to the LORD,
And He answered me.
AMP.2:
Rescue my soul, O LORD, from lying lips,
And from a deceitful tongue.
AMP.3:
What shall be given to you, and what more shall be done to you,
You deceitful tongue?—
AMP.4:
Sharp arrows of the warrior,
With the burning coals of the broom tree.
AMP.5:
Woe to me, for I sojourn in Meshech,
and I live among the tents of Kedar [among hostile people]!
AMP.6:
Too long my soul has had its dwelling
With those who hate peace.
AMP.7:
I am for peace, but when I speak,
They are for war.

MSG.1:
A pilgrim song
I'm in trouble. I cry to God,
desperate for an answer:
MSG.2:
"Deliver me from the liars, God!
They smile so sweetly but lie through their teeth."
MSG.3:
Do you know what's next, can you see what's coming,
all you barefaced liars?
MSG.4:
Pointed arrows and burning coals
will be your reward.
MSG.5:
I'm doomed to live in Meshech,
cursed with a home in Kedar,
MSG.6:
My whole life lived camping
among quarreling neighbors.
MSG.7:
I'm all for peace, but the minute
I tell them so, they go to war!

NIV.1:
Psalm 120
A song of ascents.
I call on the Lord in my distress,
and he answers me.
NIV.2:
Save me, O Lord, from lying lips
and from deceitful tongues.
NIV.3:
What will he do to you,
and what more besides, O deceitful tongue?
NIV.4:
He will punish you with a warrior’s sharp arrows,
with burning coals of the broom tree.
NIV.5:
Woe to me that I dwell in Meshech,
that I live among the tents of Kedar!
NIV.6:
Too long have I lived
among those who hate peace.
NIV.7:
I am a man of peace;
but when I speak, they are for war.

NRSVCE.1:
In my distress I cry to the LORD,
that he may answer me:
NRSVCE.2:
“Deliver me, O LORD,
from lying lips,
from a deceitful tongue.”
NRSVCE.3:
What shall be given to you?
And what more shall be done to you,
you deceitful tongue?
NRSVCE.4:
A warrior’s sharp arrows,
with glowing coals of the broom tree!
NRSVCE.5:
Woe is me, that I am an alien in Meshech,
that I must live among the tents of Kedar.
NRSVCE.6:
Too long have I had my dwelling
among those who hate peace.
NRSVCE.7:
I am for peace;
but when I speak,
they are for war.

NET.1: In my distress I cried out to the LORD and he answered me.
NET.2: I said, “O LORD, rescue me from those who lie with their lips and those who deceive with their tongue.
NET.3: How will he severely punish you, you deceptive talker?
NET.4: Here’s how! With the sharp arrows of warriors, with arrowheads forged over the hot coals.
NET.5: How miserable I am! For I have lived temporarily in Meshech; I have resided among the tents of Kedar.
NET.6: For too long I have had to reside with those who hate peace.
NET.7: I am committed to peace, but when I speak, they want to make war.
